title: Sweat on Cotton
timeEstimate: 15–45 minutes; fresh + yellow-pit forks; bounded chlorine on solid white cotton only
stainChemistry: Sweat on cotton is two stains layered together: fresh sweat (protein + skin oil + salt) which lifts easily with cool water + Dawn + enzyme, and the cumulative yellow pit stain (aluminum from antiperspirant + sweat protein) that builds over weeks and months and locks under heat. The two need different approaches. Fresh sweat: cold flush + Dawn + cool wash. Yellow pit stain: vinegar pretreat to dissolve the aluminum-sweat complex first, THEN Dawn + OxiClean + bounded peroxide. The hard rules: NO hot water (sets the protein), NO alcohol on sweat (sets the protein beyond pro recovery), NO chlorine + acid in same dwell (toxic chloramine gas). Chlorine bleach on cotton is bounded — only with care label permission, on solid white cotton with no elastane / colored trim.
whyThisWorks: Cool water keeps fresh sweat protein from setting. Dawn handles the skin-oil component. For yellow pit stains, vinegar's mild acid breaks the aluminum-sweat bond — that's the unlock that Dawn and OxiClean can't reach without it. OxiClean cool soak plus 3% peroxide handle residual yellow on white cotton. Sun-dry is a free finishing tool — UV photodegrades residual yellow on damp white cotton without any chemistry. The honest cap: years of hot-dried yellow pit buildup sometimes plateau even after the full sequence; pro reducing-bleach is the next step.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): sweat is salt + sweat proteins + urea + sebum. Aluminum chlorohydrate (in antiperspirants) forms aluminum-protein complexes that yellow under heat. Pro uses dilute acetic acid (1:5–1:10) to break the complex + NSD + 3% H₂O₂ for residual chromophore. Sodium hydrosulfite (reducing bleach) is the pro nuclear option for set yellow; chlorine bleach is bounded by elastane / trim presence.
defaultAssumption: Two-fork stain. FRESH (today's wear, damp shirt) = cool Dawn + enzyme handles it. YELLOW PIT (months / years of antiperspirant buildup on a white shirt) = full sequence with vinegar pretreat first. Modern dress shirts and athletic wear with elastane in collar / cuffs = NO chlorine bleach.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Fresh sweat (today's wear, damp) = cool Dawn + enzyme is enough. Yellow pit stain (cumulative buildup, white shirt) = vinegar pretreat FIRST, then full sequence.

spottingProtocol.technique: Flush salt and protein out the way they came in
spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold running water, white catch towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Fresh sweat only: turn inside out, hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water 30–60 sec with a white towel under the front. Cool only — heat sets the protein.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1:1 white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Yellow pit stain only: 1 part white vinegar + 1 part cool water on damp cloth, press 10–15 min. The acid breaks the aluminum-sweat bond that Dawn and OxiClean can't touch alone. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide.

spottingProtocol.agent: Dawn Pretreat — Cut the Skin Oil
spottingProtocol.instruction: Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 5–10 min. White cotton tolerates warm Dawn (warm, not hot) for the oil step. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Enzyme Pretreat (Fresh Sweat) or OxiClean Cool Soak (Yellow Pit)
spottingProtocol.equipment: Enzyme pretreat or OxiClean per label, COOL water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Fresh sweat: enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, Tide Plus) in COOL water, 20 min. Yellow pit: OxiClean cool soak 30–60 min. NEVER mix with chlorine — chlorine + alkaline OxiClean = toxic fumes.

spottingProtocol.technique: Direct peroxide pass on white cotton
spottingProtocol.equipment: 3% H₂O₂ on damp cloth or as soak additive
spottingProtocol.instruction: Yellow pit only, white cotton: 3% peroxide damp cloth 10 min, OR add 1/2 cup to OxiClean soak. NEVER mix with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or other oxidizers — toxic fumes.

spottingProtocol.agent: Cool Wash Plus Sun-Dry on White Cotton
spottingProtocol.technique: Heat-free finish, UV photodegradation for residual yellow
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Full cool cycle plus 1–3 hours sun
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cool machine wash with normal detergent. White cotton: lay damp in direct sunlight 1–3 hours — UV photodegrades residual yellow without chemistry. AIR-DRY only. Inspect dry. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

spottingProtocol.agent: Bounded Chlorine Bleach — Last Resort, Solid White Cotton Only
spottingProtocol.equipment: Chlorine bleach per label, hot wash
spottingProtocol.instruction: Bounded last resort for set yellow pit. ONLY if: (1) care label permits chlorine bleach, (2) garment is SOLID white cotton with no colored trim, (3) NO elastane / spandex / Lycra in collar / cuffs / waistband (chlorine destroys elastane permanently), (4) NO other oxidizer in the same wash. Skip if any condition is uncertain. THOROUGHLY rinse out any vinegar / peroxide first — chlorine + acid = toxic chloramine gas.

steps: 1. Identify fresh vs yellow pit fork.
2. Cold water back-flush for fresh sweat.
3. White vinegar 1:1 cool pretreat for yellow pit, 10–15 min, damp cloth.
4. Dawn pretreat warm 5–10 min for oil component.
5. Enzyme pretreat cool 20 min (fresh) OR OxiClean cool soak 30–60 min (yellow pit).
6. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth or soak additive for stubborn yellow on white cotton.
7. Cool wash + sun-dry on white cotton + AIR-DRY.
8. Bounded chlorine bleach only on solid white cotton, no elastane / trim, no oxidizer mix, vinegar fully rinsed.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile / Biz / Tide Plus / liquid Protein Spotter)
OxiClean Versatile / oxygen bleach (yellow pit soak)
Cool water for chemistry, hot wash AFTER if care label permits
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool through chemistry; hot wash AFTER protein cleared (care label permitting)
warnings: NEVER hot water on fresh sweat — sets the protein permanently
NEVER alcohol on sweat — sets protein beyond pro recovery
NEVER chlorine bleach in same dwell as vinegar / acid — toxic chloramine gas
NEVER mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + OxiClean — toxic fumes
Chlorine bleach: ONLY with care label permission AND solid white cotton AND no elastane AND no trim
Modern dress shirts almost always have elastane in collar / cuffs — chlorine destroys it
Years of hot-dried yellow pit sometimes plateau — pro reducing-bleach is next step

homeSolutions.instruction: Sweat on cotton has two cases that need different protocols. FRESH sweat (today's wear, damp shirt) = cool Dawn + enzyme handles it. YELLOW PIT STAIN (months or years of antiperspirant buildup on a white shirt) = vinegar pretreat FIRST to break the aluminum-sweat bond, then the rest of the sequence. Two hard rules apply to both: NO hot water (sets the protein) and NO alcohol on sweat (sets the protein beyond pro recovery).

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Back-Flush from the BACK (Fresh Sweat)
homeSolutions.instruction: Fresh sweat: turn the shirt inside out, hold the BACK of the spot under cold running water for 30 to 60 seconds with a white towel under the front. Cool only — even warm tap water sets the protein. Skip this step if it's set yellow pit buildup; go to step 3.

homeSolutions.agent: White Vinegar Pretreat — Yellow Pit Stain Only
homeSolutions.instruction: Yellow pit stain only: mix 1 part white vinegar with 1 part cool water. Apply with a damp cloth, press 10 to 15 minutes. Vinegar's mild acid breaks the aluminum-sweat complex that Dawn and OxiClean alone can't touch. THIS is the step that makes the rest work on yellow buildup. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before any peroxide.

Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 5 to 10 minutes. White cotton tolerates warm (warm, not hot) water for the Dawn step. Damp-cloth rinse. Sweat has skin oil mixed with the protein; Dawn cuts the oil so the next step can reach the protein underneath.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Pretreat (Fresh) or OxiClean Cool Soak (Yellow Pit)
homeSolutions.instruction: Fresh sweat: enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, or Tide Plus) in COOL water per label, 20 minutes. Yellow pit stain: OxiClean cool soak 30 to 60 minutes. Cool only — heat above bath temperature damages the enzymes AND sets the protein.

Yellow pit stain on WHITE cotton only: 3% peroxide on a damp cloth, press 10 minutes, blot. OR add 1/2 cup to the OxiClean soak. Do NOT mix with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or any other oxidizer — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.agent: Cool Wash Plus Sun-Dry on White Cotton
homeSolutions.instruction: Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY. White cotton bonus: lay the damp shirt in direct sunlight for 1 to 3 hours — UV naturally bleaches residual yellow without any chemistry (this is why old-school clotheslines kept whites brighter). Skip the sun step on colored cotton.

homeSolutions.agent: Bounded Chlorine Bleach — Check Elastane First
homeSolutions.instruction: Set yellow pit that survived the full sequence: chlorine bleach is the home nuclear option but it's BOUNDED. ONLY use chlorine if (1) the care label permits, (2) the garment is SOLID white cotton with no colored trim, (3) NO elastane / spandex / Lycra in the collar / cuffs / waistband (chlorine destroys elastane — most modern dress shirts have it; feel for stretch in the collar to test), and (4) NO other oxidizer in the same wash. THOROUGHLY rinse out any vinegar before chlorine — chlorine + acid = toxic chloramine gas. Per label dilution. Skip if any condition is uncertain.

homeSolutions.agent: Switch to Aluminum-Free Deodorant for Repeat Yellow Pit
homeSolutions.instruction: If you keep getting yellow pit stains, the cause is the aluminum in your antiperspirant reacting with sweat. Aluminum-free deodorants don't form the yellow complex — switch for shirts you want to keep white. Skip fabric softener (locks aluminum in). Let deodorant dry completely before dressing.

steps: 1. Identify fresh vs yellow pit fork.
2. NO hot water. NO alcohol on sweat. NO chlorine + acid in same dwell.
4. White vinegar 1:1 cool pretreat for yellow pit, 10–15 min, damp cloth.
5. Dawn pretreat warm 5–10 min for oil component.
6. Enzyme pretreat cool 20 min (fresh) OR OxiClean cool soak 30–60 min (yellow pit).
7. 3% peroxide damp cloth or soak additive for stubborn yellow on white cotton.
8. Cool wash + sun-dry on white cotton.
9. Bounded chlorine bleach only on solid white cotton, no elastane / trim, vinegar fully rinsed.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, Tide Plus)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on yellow pit for years (often plateau). Stop if chlorine bleach already damaged collar elastane (irreversible). Stop if alcohol-based product was used on the sweat (often sets protein). Stop after full sequence + bounded chlorine plateau.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets sweat protein permanently
NEVER alcohol on sweat — sets protein beyond pro recovery
NEVER chlorine bleach in same dwell as vinegar / acid — toxic chloramine gas
NEVER mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + OxiClean
NEVER chlorine bleach on cotton with elastane — destroys elastane permanently
Modern dress shirts almost always have elastane in collar / cuffs
Switch to aluminum-free deodorant to prevent recurrence

neverDo: Never hot water on sweat — sets protein permanently
Never alcohol on sweat — sets protein beyond pro recovery
Never chlorine bleach in same dwell as vinegar / acid — toxic chloramine gas
Never mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + OxiClean
Never chlorine bleach on cotton with elastane
Never iron over yellow pit — heat sets it
fiberSensitivities: Solid white cotton (no elastane, no trim): widest bench — vinegar, Dawn, OxiClean, 3% peroxide, sun-dry, bounded chlorine bleach
White cotton with elastane (modern dress shirts, athletic wear): NO chlorine bleach
White cotton with colored trim: NO chlorine bleach
Colored cotton: skip peroxide + chlorine; vinegar + Dawn + enzyme + cool wash sequence only
Tolerates hot wash up to 200°F AFTER chemistry

materialWarnings: Two-fork stain — fresh sweat vs yellow pit have different protocols
Vinegar is the unlock for yellow pit — without it, OxiClean and peroxide can't reach the lock
No alcohol on sweat — sets protein beyond pro recovery
Chlorine + acid = toxic chloramine gas — fully rinse vinegar before chlorine

escalation.when: Years of hot-dried yellow pit buildup that survived full sequence (often plateau). Chlorine bleach used on cotton with elastane (irreversible damage). Alcohol-based product (hand sanitizer, body spray) was applied to the spot (often sets protein). Chlorine + vinegar/peroxide mixed (toxic fume incident — ventilate, redo without mix). Cotton blend with polyester (cool-only rules).
escalation.whatToTell: "Sweat on [solid white / white-with-trim / white-with-elastane / colored / blend] cotton, [fresh / yellow pit buildup, age weeks / months / years], home-treated with [cold back-flush + vinegar pretreat (yellow pit only) + Dawn + enzyme/OxiClean + 3% peroxide + sun-dry + bounded chlorine if eligible]. No hot, no alcohol. [Residual yellow / collar elastane damaged / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional wet cleaner with reducing-bleach (sodium hydrosulfite) chemistry for set yellow pit

contextQuestions: Fresh sweat (today's wear) or yellow pit stain (months / years of buildup)?
Solid white cotton, white with colored trim, white with elastane, or colored?
Has the garment been hot-dried with the yellow buildup?
Has alcohol-based product (hand sanitizer, body spray) been applied to the spot?
Care label — hot-wash and chlorine-bleach permitted?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: The two-fork structure (fresh vs yellow pit) is the entire job. Fresh sweat is easy; yellow pit stain is the cumulative aluminum-sweat lock and needs the vinegar pretreat to break the bond before any cleaning step works. Aluminum-free deodorant is the prevention path — eliminates recurrence. Modern dress shirts and athletic wear almost always contain elastane in the collar / cuffs / waistband, which chlorine destroys permanently — chlorine option is bounded. Alcohol pre-applied (hand sanitizer, body spray) is the irreversible-failure mode that sets the protein.
